summary: Iteration 57 - Inventory Module Batch 5 Testing. Backend APIs work correctly (11/11 tests passed). Frontend has CRITICAL RBAC bug: ERPActionToolbar buttons don't show because user.role_id is NULL causing 'Role not found' error. StockCards toolbar works (custom buttons without ERPActionToolbar). Stock operations (in/out/delete) work via API.

minor.issue: Kasir can create stock opname (returns 200) - should return 403 for RBAC

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: All buttons (Tambah, Edit, Hapus, Print) don't show on StockMovements, StockTransfers, StockOpname pages
Toolbar container visible but empty (0 buttons) due to hasPermission() returning false

integration_issues.issue: User role_id is NULL in database, causing /api/rbac/user/permissions to return 'Role not found' error

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: User ocbgroupbjm@gmail.com has role='owner' but role_id=NULL - need to assign proper role_id UUID
Update auth/login to ensure role_id is always set when creating/updating users
Alternative fix: Update get_user_permissions() to fallback to role lookup by role code if role_id is null
RBAC: Review POST /api/inventory/opnames permission check for kasir role
critical_code_review_comments: User data has role='owner' (text) but role_id=null - data integrity issue
rbac_system.py line 378: looks for role by role_id which is null, should fallback to role code lookup
StockCards.jsx uses custom toolbar (Refresh/Print/Export) which works fine - not using ERPActionToolbar
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend inventory APIs fully functional. Frontend RBAC system broken - hasPermission() always returns false because role_id is null. Fix the user's role_id assignment first, then retest ERPActionToolbar buttons on all inventory pages.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: User record has role='owner' (string) but role_id=null (missing UUID). The RBAC permission endpoint /api/rbac/user/permissions looks up role by role_id which fails.
reproduction_steps: 1. Login as ocbgroupbjm@gmail.com with db ocb_unt_1
3. Open browser console - see 'Permission error: Role not found'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Either: (1) Fix user's role_id in database to point to valid role UUID, OR (2) Update get_user_permissions() in rbac_system.py to fallback to role lookup by role code
